codeReview.summary: The commit improves two important pieces of the feature: browser hash/back-forward focus restoration in `GraphShell` and an obvious proof→graph handoff via the proof CTA plus the hero’s proof-first primary CTA. But the implementation still does not preserve the applicable sub-surface context that the feature explicitly promises. `GraphShell` swaps the active surface subtree in and out, so leaving Tokenomics or Roadmap unmounts the stateful components that own scenario inputs and expanded-branch state. Returning to those surfaces recreates them from defaults, which breaks cross-surface context preservation for scenarios and roadmap exploration state.

issues.description: `ScenarioSwitcher` keeps the selected scenario and user inputs in local component state (`activeLevel`, `tokenBalance`, `depositAmount`), while `GraphShell` only renders the currently focused surface (`src/components/graph/GraphShell.tsx:315-384`). Navigating from Tokenomics to another graph surface unmounts this subtree, so browser back/forward or node-to-node handoffs recreate Tokenomics with the default `base` scenario and blank inputs instead of preserving the feature's required cross-surface scenario context.

`RoadmapAtlas` stores `expandedBranches` in local reducer state initialized from an empty object, and `GraphShell` unmounts the roadmap surface whenever focus moves to another node (`src/components/graph/GraphShell.tsx:315-384`). That means multi-branch exploration state is discarded on cross-surface handoffs; at best a hashed selected node can reopen one family, but previously expanded sibling branches are silently lost, which misses the feature's expanded-branch preservation requirement.

The new regression suite overstates its coverage for the missing behaviors. The VAL-CROSS-003 test mounts a placeholder `<div>` for the tokenomics surface instead of the real `ScenarioSwitcher` (`lines 119-122`), and the 'sub-surface content' test only swaps mock surfaces (`lines 270-277`). As a result, the added tests never exercise scenario/input persistence or roadmap branch-state preservation across real surface handoffs.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Procedure-adherence evidence for frontend shell work is still too weak: this worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true`, but the handoff does not satisfy the skill's explicit requirement to cite at least one newly failing test expectation written before the implementation change.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/test/vision/.factory/skills/frontend-experience-worker/SKILL.md:28 requires workers to write failing tests first and cite at least one newly failing test expectation in the handoff. In /Users/test/.factory/missions/33dae310-26cf-4368-9488-64b235b6db83/handoffs/2026-03-22T08-11-49-621Z__graph-shell-cross-surface-handoffs-and-context__7e5038a1-a639-426d-bfd0-71080225fcc1.json:75-139, the handoff lists added tests and marks `followedProcedure` true, but it never names any failing pre-implementation expectation.

summary: Fail. The proof-first CTA flow and hash/back-forward focus restoration are solid improvements, but the feature still drops two applicable pieces of required cross-surface context: tokenomics scenario/input state and roadmap expanded-branch state both reset when users leave those surfaces and return. I also noted a skill-adherence evidence gap around the required test-first handoff proof.
